reviews.review_msg: Lydia is NOT A HEALTH SCIENCE PROFESSOR!!!! She is a PHILOSOPHY Prof and it really showed. This class was not made to be relevant to health science majors. Readings (mandatory for assignment completion) are HEAVY. Long and complex. Like read 3 times to understand complex. No midterm and final though..!

Lydia means well and is a very nice and understanding prof, but her lectures were brutal. After week 4, 5-10 students attended lecture which made it difficult to attend + sit in the quiet room. The lecture, tutorial and assignments were all pretty irrelevant to each other, they felt like 3 different courses. The TA (Ravi) made this class bearable.

Her lectures are honestly some of the most interesting I've ever attended. She has this incredible way of making complex ideas super relatable and engaging. You can tell she puts so much effort into her presentations and the reading materials.  If you get the chance to study with Lidya, don't pass it up!

Her lectures are honestly some of the most interesting I've ever attended. She has this incredible way of making complex ideas super relatable and engaging. You can tell she puts so much effort into her presentations and the reading materials. 
If you get the chance to study with Lidya, don't pass it up!

Her lectures can be a bit monotone, but she is a decent prof overall who's unfairly given a bad rep. Interesting readings, consistent workload throughout the semester, clear instructions. Records all lecs and she also records content when she cannot make a lecture for fam reasons (not like we are losing out on material). Overall would take again!

This professor is for starters, not very captivating during her lectures. I genuinely only struggled with this aspect of the class. However, I found it so disrespectful that she would cancel lectures with so little care about our time and money.
Why does she never show up to her own lectures? She cancelled 5/13 lectures with little notice. SFU should be ashamed to have such poor professors.

Lydia has completely disrespected the time, money, and effort that students have dedicated to their studies. She cancelled three lectures before week seven. In terms of the timing of her cancellations, she would wait until extremely late the night before (12:30 - 2:00 am) or only an hour before the lecture was scheduled. Not impressed.

Awesome class, cool assignments; an all-round first class prof! Highly recommend Lydia's classes, she keeps them entertaining and grounded to the realities of research /philosophy/academia in general. Her courses are fantastic for ensuring all students fully grasp the material and leave better prepared to comprehend heavy texts.

This rating is for PHIL 343 but it was not an option. Lydia is a pretty good professor. Lectures can occasionally feel a bit dry, but she gives clear instructions for her assignments/papers/final and definitely cares about students' success. She's also super accommodating, which is refreshing.

I don't even know where to begin.   Avoid this professor like the plague. Not only did I learn nothing in this class, I struggled to keep myself awake every lecture. I am not even sure what this class is about. I took no notes and the selected readings from the textbook just confused me more. Is it that difficult to find Philosophy professors?!?!?!

I don't know how someone can take a topic as interesting as philosophy of science and make me want to cry from boredom every class. There is no direction and she will elaborate on pretty menial parts of the textbook for an hour. She also has never responded to a single e-mail that I've ever sent her (despite her reassurances that she will).

If you want to see how such a witty and brilliant professor as Lydia can teach so boring a class, enroll.  Lectures are unorganized and lack direction, but you can really tell that she is meticulous when you realize one hour into the class all she has talked about was something minuscule. I learned nothing from this class.
